name: Vulkan Smoke Default And Lavapipe Caveat

messages.content:
I’ll run a fast-forward pull on `main` and check the working tree first, since there may now be a local source edit in addition to the existing `.cozter` files.

Only `.cozter` metadata/upload files are currently dirty; no first-party source files are modified. I’m running `git pull --ff-only` now.

The pull fast-forwarded successfully. I’ll check status once more so the final branch and local changes are explicit.

Pulled `main` successfully with a fast-forward:

`a171daf -> 7161974`

Upstream changed 4 Vulkan files. The working tree still has only `.cozter` metadata/upload changes; no first-party source files are dirty.

I’ll run another fast-forward pull on `main`. First I’m checking the current branch and dirty set so any local metadata files are clear before updating.

The working tree is dirty only under `.cozter`, including one uploaded image. I’m proceeding with `git pull --ff-only`; it will stop if upstream touches the same paths.

The pull fast-forwarded successfully and brought in Vulkan graphics changes. I’m checking final status now so the remaining local changes are explicit.

Pulled `main` successfully with a fast-forward:

`7161974 -> fabd327`

Upstream changed 9 Vulkan graphics files. The working tree still has only `.cozter` metadata/upload changes; no first-party source files are dirty.

Caveat: the optional Mesa lavapipe software Vulkan path still crashes during first render. GDB shows the crash inside libvulkan_lvp.so / libLLVM.so.22.1 during sampler/shader work, not in project code. Hardware Vulkan is fine.

about this, do we have something to do

I’ll check how the Vulkan backend chooses devices and how tests handle software ICDs. The useful distinction is whether lavapipe is an expected supported path or just an optional CI/developer fallback we should detect and skip.

There is already a smoke-test knob for lavapipe: `--software-vulkan` defaults to “auto” and sets `VK_ICD_FILENAMES` to the Mesa LVP ICD when present. I’m checking whether the runtime itself also records enough device identity to gate or diagnose that path.

The backend already logs selected device name/type/vendor when debug runtime is enabled, but selection treats CPU devices as acceptable fallback. Since the smoke script actively prefers lavapipe in `auto`, this caveat can affect tests even when hardware Vulkan is healthy.

There is one concrete repo-side fix worth making: the smoke test currently forces lavapipe whenever it is installed, because `--software-vulkan` defaults to `auto`. I’m going to make hardware/system ICD the default and leave lavapipe as an explicit opt-in for environments that still want it.

The change is intentionally narrow: it only changes the smoke script default and help text. I’m checking the diff and doing a Python syntax/whitespace pass now.

Yes: the repo-side action is to stop forcing the broken optional path.

I changed [tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py:1359) so `--software-vulkan` now defaults to `off` instead of `auto`. That means CTest/manual smoke runs will use the system Vulkan ICD, so hardware Vulkan stays on the normal path. Lavapipe is still available explicitly with `--software-vulkan auto` or `--software-vulkan on`.

Given the crash is inside `libvulkan_lvp.so` / `libLLVM.so.22.1` and hardware Vulkan is fine, I would not add renderer workarounds unless validation layers show our Vulkan usage is invalid. The practical next step for lavapipe support would be a Mesa bug/repro, not project code.

Verified:
- `python3 -m py_compile tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py`
- `python3 tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py --help`
- `git diff --check`

summary:
Ran multiple `git pull --ff-only` fast-forwards on `main` while keeping the working tree dirty only under `.cozter/` (metadata plus one uploaded image); no first‑party source files were left modified after the Vulkan optimization work. After the earlier `63fee70 -> 01dc380` FF, two additional FF pulls landed: `a171daf -> 7161974` (upstream changed 4 Vulkan files) and `7161974 -> fabd327` (upstream changed 9 Vulkan graphics files). Each time, `git status` confirmed only `.cozter` paths were modified locally.

Investigated a reported Mesa lavapipe (software Vulkan) crash during first render (GDB shows crash in `libvulkan_lvp.so` / `libLLVM.so.22.1`, hardware Vulkan OK). Implemented a narrow repo-side mitigation by changing the smoke script default to stop auto-forcing lavapipe: updated `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py` so `--software-vulkan` now defaults to `off` (lavapipe remains opt-in via `auto`/`on`). Verified with `python3 -m py_compile`, `--help`, and `git diff --check`.
long_term: NWBLot is a C++/CMake project built via presets `linux-clang-{dbg,opt,fin}`, `linux-clang-engine-{dbg,opt,fin}`, and `linux-clang-testbed-{dbg,opt,fin}`, with tests run via `ctest` from the generated `__cmake/build/...` trees.
Default scope is first‑party source only; avoid touching `3rd_parties/`, generated assets, and build output unless explicitly requested.
Many C++ sources require CRLF; preserve line endings/EOF conventions on touched files and use `git diff --check` to ensure whitespace hygiene.
Follow the project coding standard in `.helper/standard.md`, including using project wrappers and scratch/cache‑aligned allocation patterns for temporaries where appropriate.
Prefer iterative cleanup cycles: scan → defensible fixes → build/test → rescan until no worthwhile first‑party changes remain.
Static analyzers like `clang-tidy`, `cppcheck`, and `scan-build` are not available; rely on compiler builds/tests and targeted checks instead.
Graphical/smoke tests may flake under parallel full-suite runs; sequential reruns are used to confirm regressions vs timing.
Engine/testbed preset builds can emit a recoverable Ninja “premature end of file; recovering” warning without failing the build.
Project metadata requires explicit `asset.geometry_class` and rejects unsupported/old binary versions without migration shims.
The GitHub CLI `gh` is not available here; publishing is done via direct commits on `main` and `git push origin main`.
Prefer `git pull --ff-only` on `main` to avoid accidental merge commits.

Some project types (e.g., `Float44`) cannot be default-inserted via `std::vector::resize(count)` under project allocators, so “exact-size then fill” optimizations must be guarded (e.g., by default-constructibility) or avoided.
`global/binary.h` binary vector payload reads skip redundant `initialValue` fills only when the payload type is default-constructible, using `global/type_borrow.h` trait wrappers.
Metascript `Value` enforces arena ownership across cross-arena copy/move and list operations, with regression coverage under `tests/metascript/`.
`.cozter/` workspace metadata is normally kept out of commits, but can be committed when explicitly requested.
The smoke test script `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py` uses `--software-vulkan` to control Mesa lavapipe usage via `VK_ICD_FILENAMES`, and its default is now `off` so lavapipe is opt-in.
Mesa lavapipe (software Vulkan) may crash during first render in some environments with the failure occurring inside Mesa/LLVM rather than project code, while hardware Vulkan remains fine.
